title: [VMIC-A4] Settings UI virtual microphone picker
scope: Settings editor exposes TTS routing as a choice field and virtual_mic_device as a keyboard-selectable virtual endpoint picker, persists both fields, and preserves API key masking.

test_cases.config_editor_cycles_tts_routing_choices: pass - F2/Ctrl+D cycles speakers -> virtual_mic -> both -> speakers
test_cases.config_editor_cycles_only_detected_virtu: pass - picker cycles only configured virtual endpoint options
test_cases.config_editor_virtual_mic_cycle_explains: pass - empty probe shows an install/reopen Settings recovery message
test_cases.build_config_from_editor_persists_tts_ro: pass - route enum and virtual_mic_device are copied from editor state into AppConfig
test_cases.build_config_from_editor_rejects_invalid: pass - invalid route values fail with a tts_routing validation message
test_cases.config_save_persists_tts_route_and_virtu: pass - saved config round-trips virtual_mic route and device
test_cases.config_editor_onboarding_navigation_skip: pass - onboarding Tab/Shift+Tab wraps across only visible fields after advanced pipeline knobs are hidden
test_cases.settings_tts_route_selection_persists_ke: pass - PTY opens Settings, tabs to TTS routing, sends Ctrl+D, saves, and verifies config.json

commands.name: snapshot-update
commands.summary: 329 snapshot tests passed; four config-editor snapshots updated

commands.name: full-tests
commands.summary: All Rust test targets passed, including PTY and snapshot suites

commands.name: windows-release-build
commands.note: A stale tui-translator.exe process was holding the release binary; stopped only PID 192984 before rerunning the release build.

notes: Settings population now probes both normal capture devices and virtual audio endpoints when the editor opens.
Virtual mic F2/Ctrl+D selection does not invent a blank/default route target; clearing remains possible by editing the field manually.
Onboarding hides advanced pipeline tuning rows so compact and full onboarding panels keep keyboard hints visible after adding route fields.
Onboarding keyboard navigation skips the hidden advanced pipeline fields so Tab/Shift+Tab never lands on an invisible editable field.
AppConfig validation remains the single source of truth for invalid virtual_mic_device/tts_routing combinations.
